tipRules.tip: Yamamoto pro Hawk: 'Insert nail weight in thick end of Senko, pin hook in middle. Weight in head makes it fall straight down very fast — fish this rig easily in 15-45 feet on 4-6lb line. Drag, shake softly, fall technique'

Japanese pro Iwahori tip (from Yamamoto ranch): Insert nail weight in head but position hook in-line with bait (shank facing forward) instead of perpendicular — allows fishing through vegetation with far less snagging
FLW pro Casey Martin: Rig 3/16-oz shaky head with 5-inch stick bait — stands upright for enticing look. Pitch around docks or fish over deeper structure
Nose hook or Texas rig a 3-inch stickbait for simple baitfish profile. For power shotting, upsize to 4-5 inch wacky rigged for maximum motion
FLW pro Casey Martin: Pair stick bait with monofilament leader on C-rig — lets bait float above short grass on ledges and offshore points for appearance of vulnerable baitfish
Elite pro Scroggins: Keep unweighted 5-inch Senko handy when topwater fishing around shallow vegetation — if a fish misses your topwater, throw the Senko at point of contact for quick followup bite
Using a stick bait as swim jig trailer shows finicky fish a more streamlined profile
Using a stick bait as vibrating jig trailer shows finicky fish a more streamlined profile — something different

knowledge.insight: Weighted wacky hooks and wacky jig heads (flick shake) take the double-ended wiggle straight to the bottom. FLW pro Roy Hawk: 'Throw it at anything — grass lines, pockets, docks, single rocks, shade lines. Vary the head weight depending on wind, current and depth'
FLW pro Roy Hawk prefers nail-weighted Neko over flick shake in deeper waters because the rig falls faster with less lateral resistance — straight down

Rubbing the tail of a Senko releases more salt and subtly changes the color — an on-the-fly alteration when fish have seen enough of one color. Chartreuse tail dip gets most carries; orange works where bluegill abound
Kevin Hawk: Owner Flashy accent (Indiana or willow blade on barrel swivel slipped onto hook bend) used in murky water or high pressure situations to draw more attention to wacky-rigged Senko
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends
